questions.text: What is the opening warning of Surah Al-Anbiya regarding mankind's judgment?
questions.answer: That the account (judgment) for mankind has drawn near, yet they turn away in heedlessness.

questions.text: How do the heedless people react to the new reminders from their Lord?
questions.answer: They listen to them while they are playing, their hearts distracted, and they say the message is nothing but 'confused dreams' or magic.

questions.text: Were the messengers sent before Muhammad (SAW) angels or humans?
questions.answer: They were men to whom Allah sent revelation; they were not bodies that did not eat food, nor were they immortal.

questions.text: What is the response to the claim that the heavens and earth could have multiple gods?
questions.answer: If there were gods other than Allah in the heavens and earth, they both would have been ruined in chaos.

questions.text: Can any of the creation ask Allah about what He does?
questions.answer: No, He is not questioned about what He does, but they will be questioned.

questions.text: What was the message given to every messenger before the Prophet Muhammad (SAW)?
questions.answer: That there is no deity except Allah, so worship Him alone.

questions.text: What is the refutation for those who say the Most Merciful has taken a son?
questions.answer: Allah is exalted! Rather, those they call sons (like angels or prophets) are but honored servants who do not precede Him in speech and act by His command.

questions.text: Describe the concept of the heavens and earth being a 'joined entity' as mentioned in this Surah.
questions.answer: The heavens and the earth were a joined entity (Ratqan) and Allah separated them (Fataqnahuma).

questions.text: What is the origin of all living things according to this Surah?
questions.answer: Water.

questions.text: What is the function of mountains on the earth according to this Surah?
questions.answer: They are placed as firm stabilizers so that the earth does not shake with its inhabitants.

questions.text: What is the 'protected ceiling' mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: The sky/atmosphere, yet the disbelievers turn away from its signs.

questions.text: Who created the night, the day, the sun, and the moon, and how do they move?
questions.answer: Allah created them, and each is swimming in an orbit.

questions.text: Has Allah granted immortality to any human being before the Prophet Muhammad (SAW)?
questions.answer: No, and if the Prophet were to die, the disbelievers would not be immortal either.

questions.text: How does Allah describe every soul's destiny regarding death and trial?
questions.answer: Every soul shall taste death, and Allah tests people with evil and good as a trial, and to Him they will be returned.

questions.text: How did the disbelievers mock the Prophet when they saw him?
questions.answer: They said, 'Is this the one who mentions your gods (mockingly)?' while they themselves disbelieved in the mention of the Most Merciful.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the creation of man in terms of his nature?
questions.answer: Man was created of haste (instinctive impatience).

questions.text: What will the disbelievers wish they could do when the Fire envelops their faces and backs?
questions.answer: They will wish they could ward it off, but it will come upon them unexpectedly and they will not be able to repel it.

questions.text: What happens to those who mock the messengers of Allah?
questions.answer: They become surrounded by the very thing they used to mock (the punishment).

questions.text: Who can protect people by night or by day from the Most Merciful?
questions.answer: No one; yet they turn away from the remembrance of their Lord.

questions.text: What does Allah say about the 'balance' of deeds on Judgment Day?
questions.answer: He will place the scales of justice, and no soul will be treated unjustly even by the weight of a mustard seed.

questions.text: What is the 'Al-Furqan' given to Musa and Harun?
questions.answer: The Criterion (the Torah) which was a light and a reminder for the righteous.

questions.text: How is the Quran described in relation to previous reminders?
questions.answer: As a blessed reminder that Allah has sent down.

questions.text: What did Ibrahim (AS) ask his father and his people about their statues?
questions.answer: He asked, 'What are these statues to which you are devoted?'

questions.text: What was the people's excuse for worshipping idols?
questions.answer: They said, 'We found our fathers worshipping them.'

questions.text: What did Ibrahim (AS) do to the idols when his people were away?
questions.answer: He smashed them into pieces, except for the largest one, so they might return to it.

questions.text: What was the people's reaction when they saw their broken idols?
questions.answer: They said, 'Who has done this to our gods? Indeed, he is of the wrongdoers,' and they brought Ibrahim before the people.

questions.text: How did Ibrahim (AS) mock the logic of his people regarding the broken idols?
questions.answer: He told them to ask the big idol if it could speak, forcing them to admit that idols cannot speak.

questions.text: What did the people decide to do to Ibrahim (AS) after being humiliated in argument?
questions.answer: They said, 'Burn him and support your gods!'

questions.text: How was Ibrahim (AS) saved from the fire?
questions.answer: Allah commanded the fire: 'O fire, be coolness and safety upon Ibrahim'.

questions.text: To which land did Allah deliver Ibrahim (AS) and Lut (AS)?
questions.answer: To the land which He had blessed for the worlds (the Levant/Sham).

questions.text: What offspring did Allah grant Ibrahim (AS) as a gift?
questions.answer: Ishaq (Isaac) and Ya'qub (Jacob) as an additional gift, and He made all of them righteous.

questions.text: What was the specific sin of the people of Lut (AS) mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: They were doing 'evil deeds' (immorality), and Allah saved Lut from that city of wicked people.

questions.text: Briefly describe the story of Nuh (AS) mentioned in this Surah.
questions.answer: He called upon Allah before the others, and Allah responded to him and saved him and his family from the great distress.

questions.text: What was the 'judgment' case that Dawud (AS) and Sulayman (AS) were deciding?
questions.answer: A case where the sheep of some people had strayed and grazed in another person's field at night.

questions.text: Which of the two, Dawud or Sulayman, was given the deeper understanding of the judgment?
questions.answer: Sulayman (AS), although Allah gave both of them judgment and knowledge.

questions.text: What did the mountains and birds do along with Dawud (AS)?
questions.answer: They exalted Allah (did Tasbih) along with him.

questions.text: What 'craft' did Allah teach Dawud (AS) for the benefit of people?
questions.answer: The making of coats of mail (armor) to protect them in battle.

questions.text: What power did Allah give Sulayman (AS) over the wind?
questions.answer: The wind was made subservient to him, blowing violently by his command toward the land He had blessed.

questions.text: Who else was made subservient to Sulayman (AS) to dive and perform other tasks?
questions.answer: Devils (Shayatin) from among the Jinn.

questions.text: What was the 'Cry' of Prophet Ayyub (Job) in his distress?
questions.answer: 'Indeed, adversity has touched me, and You are the Most Merciful of the merciful'.

questions.text: How did Allah respond to Ayyub's (AS) prayer?
questions.answer: He removed his adversity and gave him back his family and the like of them with them, as a mercy and a reminder.

questions.text: Who are 'Dhul-Kifl', 'Idris', and 'Isma'il' described as in this Surah?
questions.answer: They were all among the patient ones (Sabirin) and Allah admitted them into His mercy.

questions.text: What is the title given to Yunus (AS) in this Surah?
questions.answer: Dhun-Nun (The Man of the Whale/Fish).

questions.text: What was the 'Dua' of Yunus (AS) inside the belly of the whale?
questions.answer: La ilaha illa Anta, Subhanaka, inni kuntu minaz-zalimin (There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers).

questions.text: Why did Allah respond to Zakariyya's (AS) prayer for a son?
questions.answer: Because they used to hasten to good deeds, call upon Allah in hope and fear, and were humble before Him.

questions.text: How is Maryam (AS) described in terms of her chastity and the miracle of Isa (AS)?
questions.answer: She guarded her chastity, so Allah breathed into her through His Spirit and made her and her son a sign for the worlds.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the 'Ummah' (community) of the prophets?
questions.answer: As one single community, and He is their only Lord, so they should worship Him.

questions.text: What happens to the unity of the community over time according to this Surah?
questions.answer: People divided their affair among themselves into sects, but all will return to Allah.

questions.text: Can a person's righteous effort be denied if they are a believer?
questions.answer: No, 'whoever does righteous deeds while he is a believer—no denial will there be for his effort.'

questions.text: What will happen when Gog and Magog (Ya'juj and Ma'juj) are let loose?
questions.answer: They will swarm down from every elevation, and the 'True Promise' will draw near.

questions.text: What will the disbelievers say when the Day of Judgment arrives?
questions.answer: 'O woe to us; we were in heedlessness of this; rather, we were wrongdoers'.

questions.text: What will be the 'fuel' of Hell according to this Surah?
questions.answer: The disbelievers and what they used to worship besides Allah.

questions.text: What will be the state of the righteous who were promised good by Allah?
questions.answer: They will be kept far away from Hell; they will not hear its slightest sound and will abide in what their souls desire.

questions.text: Will the 'Great Terror' of the Last Day grieve the righteous?
questions.answer: No, and the angels will meet them, saying, 'This is your Day which you were promised.'

questions.text: What will happen to the heavens on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: Allah will fold the heaven like the folding of a scroll for records.

questions.text: What was written in the 'Zabur' (Psalms) regarding the inheritance of the earth?
questions.answer: That 'My righteous servants shall inherit the earth'.

questions.text: How does Allah describe Prophet Muhammad (SAW) in this Surah?
questions.answer: As a 'Mercy to the worlds' (Rahmatan lil-'Alamin).

questions.text: What is the core message the Prophet is told to announce to the people?
questions.answer: That your God is only one God; so will you then be Muslims (submitting to Him)?

questions.text: What should the Prophet say if the people turn away after being warned?
questions.answer: 'I have announced [the message] to all of you equally. and I do not know if what you are promised is near or far.'

questions.text: What is the final prayer/statement of the Prophet mentioned at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: 'My Lord, judge [between us] in truth. And our Lord is the Most Merciful, whose help is sought against that which you describe.'

questions.text: What is the meaning of the word 'Ratq' and 'Fatq' in the context of creation?
questions.answer: Ratq means being joined or closed, and Fatq means splitting or opening up.

questions.text: Why does Allah mention the 'orbit' of celestial bodies?
questions.answer: To show the perfect order and design in the universe as a sign of His power.

questions.text: What is the 'account' (Hisab) that is drawing near?
questions.answer: The Day of Resurrection when everyone will be judged for their deeds.

questions.text: Why are prophets called 'men' in this Surah?
questions.answer: To refute the idea that a messenger must be a supernatural being or an angel.

questions.text: What is the 'confused dreams' accusation?
questions.answer: A dismissal by the disbelievers of the Prophet's revelation as mere sub-conscious thoughts.

questions.text: What is the 'shattering' of cities?
questions.answer: The divine destruction of nations that persisted in wrongdoing.

questions.text: What is the 'amusement' the heavens and earth were NOT created for?
questions.answer: Playing or pointlessness; they were created with a serious purpose.

questions.text: What is the 'truth' that hurls itself against falsehood?
questions.answer: The divine message that exposes and destroys lies.

questions.text: Who are the ones who 'do not tire' of worshipping Allah?
questions.answer: The angels who are in His presence.

questions.text: What is the 'witness' of the Prophet for his people?
questions.answer: His testimony on the Day of Judgment regarding whether he delivered the message.

questions.text: What is the 'remembrance' (Dhikr) mentioned in the context of Musa?
questions.answer: The Torah as a source of guidance.

questions.text: Why was the largest idol left intact by Ibrahim?
questions.answer: To challenge the people to ask it for information, exposing its inability to act.

questions.text: What is the 'coolness and safety' of the fire?
questions.answer: The miracle where the physical properties of fire were changed by Allah's command.

questions.text: What is the 'blessed land' mentioned multiple times?
questions.answer: The region of Palestine and surrounding areas where many prophets lived.

questions.text: What is the 'good news' given to Zakariyya?
questions.answer: The birth of Yahya despite all physical odds.

questions.text: What is the 'Great Distress' (Al-Karb al-Azim)?
questions.answer: The flood that destroyed the people of Nuh.

questions.text: What is the 'understanding' given to Sulayman?
questions.answer: A specific wisdom in resolving complex human disputes.

questions.text: What is the 'adversity' of Ayyub?
questions.answer: His long period of illness and loss of wealth and family.

questions.text: What is the 'darkness' in which Yunus called out?
questions.answer: The darkness of the night, the sea, and the belly of the whale.

questions.text: What is the 'True Promise' (Al-Wa'd al-Haqq)?
questions.answer: The Day of Resurrection.
